body.u: I remember it like it was yesterday. I was sixteen at the time and just leaving school. I sent away for an apprenticeship with the Royal Navy, and got in. When I left school in June. I went straight into the Navy doing an apprenticeship as an electrician. At the age of 19 I had finished my apprenticeship and went aboard a ship. Here I served what was left of my time and when I left at 25, I’d travelled all round the world. At the Age of 26, I had got me self a place in London. At the age of 27 I would get myself a motorbike and {toured?} the country. At the age of about 30 I’d settle down and have got married. I’d got myself a job as electrician by now and had a steady job. Then then moved to another country. We lived in United States for quite a time before we moved back to England. I was thirty three when we had our first child, and it was a boy. At the age of forty we all moved back to England and I was forty. My wife died when I was fifty and I went into an old people’s home. I remarried at the age of fifty five and we both lived in a flat in London. We moved out when I was fifty nine and moved into my old people’s home because of my wife’s bad health, where we are still living at the age of 60.
